DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

Heavy fog is expected to settle across the district after sunset. Streetlamps may struggle to cut through the haze, and visibility around the older blocks will be significantly reduced.

Residents are advised to stay on familiar routes. Reports of distant voices carrying through the fog continue to increase, though officials insist this is a natural acoustic phenomenon.

If you hear footsteps behind you, do not assume they belong to someone you can see.

DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

Steady rainfall is expected throughout the evening. Water accumulation has already been reported near the lower streets, and several alleyways may become difficult to navigate after dark.

Local businesses are expected to remain open, though foot traffic will likely be lighter than usual.

Rain has a way of uncovering things the city tried to hide.

DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

Light drizzle is expected across most of the district tonight. Conditions remain calm, though persistent moisture may create slick pavement near older construction zones.

The atmosphere is expected to remain quiet, with most residents choosing to stay indoors.

Some nights the city whispers instead of speaking.

DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

Dense cloud cover will remain overhead through the night. No major precipitation is expected, though sunlight has been officially declared missing for another day.

Businesses report normal activity levels despite the unusually gloomy conditions.

The sky has been gray for so long that few remember what color it used to be.

DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

Strong winds are expected throughout the evening, particularly near elevated walkways and abandoned construction sites.

Loose signs, paper notices, and unsecured objects may be carried across multiple streets.

The wind keeps stealing things. Names. Notes. Sometimes entire conversations.

DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

Thunderstorms are expected to move across the district after nightfall. Residents are encouraged to avoid isolated streets and remain indoors whenever possible.

Power interruptions may occur in older sectors. Emergency crews have been placed on standby.

Every storm leaves the district slightly different than before.

DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

Unusually clear skies are expected tonight. Visibility will remain excellent throughout the district, and conditions are expected to stay calm until morning.

Many residents have already gathered on rooftops and balconies to enjoy the rare weather.

On nights like this, the city almost forgets what it is.

DISTRICT FORECAST BUREAU

A dense layer of mist has been reported spreading from the outskirts toward the city center. Visibility may drop to only a few meters in some areas.

Officials advise residents not to travel alone after dark.

Several reports received last night described unfamiliar landmarks appearing briefly before disappearing again.

If a street does not look the way you remember it, turn around.
